DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-01-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lee Hepner, Esq., 415.912.5950, for PLAINTIFF BOB PLANTHOLD.
Nelson Richards, Deputy Attorney General, 415.703.5559, for DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. The state is not a person under 42 USC 1983 and therefore, the first and third causes of action fail as a matter of law. See Will v. Mich. Dept. of State Police (1989) 491 U.S. 58, 64. The remaining causes of action fail because plaintiff fails to show that the State can provide plaintiff any relief. Lastly, plaintiff failed to provide any substantive opposition to the State's demurrer. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR5976

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-01-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lee Hepner, Esq., 415.912.5950, for PLAINTIFF BOB PLANTHOLD.
Cara Jenkins, Esq., 916.341.8240, for Defendants SENATE COMMITTEE ON TRANSPORTATION AND HOUSING, CALIFORNIA STATE SENATE, CALIFORNIA STATE ASSEMBLY, SENATOR MARK DESAULNIER, ASSEMBLYMEMBER ISADORE HALL, III, SENATE COMMITTEE ON RULES.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS SENATE COMMITTEE ON TRANSPORTATION AND HOUSING, CALIFORNIA STATE SENATE, CALIFORNIA STATE ASSEMBLY, SENATOR MARK DESAULNIER, ASSEMBLYMEMBER ISADORE HALL, III, SENATE COMMITTEE ON RULES' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to the entire amended complaint. The Legislature has plenary authority over its internal rules. See People's Advocate, Inc. v. Sup Crt. (1986) 181 Cal.App.3d 316, 322; Ex parte D. O. McCarthy (1866) 29 Cal. 395, 403. The Legislature did not violate plaintiff's due process or freedom of speech rights by suspending its internal rules and enacting Assembly Concurrent Resolution No. 65. Procedural due process principles apply to adjudicative acts, not legislative ones. See Horn v. County of Ventura (1979) 24 Cal.3d 605. Plaintiff fails to allege how he has a property interest in the Bay Bridge or how the Legislature violated plaintiff's freedom of speech rights. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR5976

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
